questions.question: Where should we store transcript files on KVM - in the database as text content (like artifacts) or as files on disk with static file serving?

options.label: Database (inline content)
options.description: Store transcript content as TEXT in database. Simple, no file I/O, automatic versioning. Good for transcripts under ~1MB.

options.label: Filesystem (disk storage)
options.description: Store as files in /opt/stack/AIStudio/storage/transcripts/. Better for large files, requires Express static serving setup.

questions.question: When should transcripts be copied from laptop to KVM?

options.label: Immediately on completion
options.description: Copy transcript when agent completes (record_agent_complete). Proactive, transcripts always available, but uses more storage.

options.label: On-demand viewing
options.description: Copy only when user clicks 'View Transcript'. Saves storage, but requires user to wait for transfer.

questions.question: How should transcripts be displayed in the UI?

options.label: Raw JSONL format
options.description: Show the raw transcript file as-is. Simple, preserves all data, but less user-friendly.

options.label: Parsed and formatted
options.description: Parse JSONL and display conversation turns, tool calls, and responses in a readable format. Better UX, requires parsing logic.

questions.question: What size transcripts do you expect? This affects storage and transfer approach.

options.label: Small (<100KB)
options.description: Typical short sessions. Database storage is fine.

options.label: Large (1MB-10MB+)
options.description: Long sessions or many tool calls. Filesystem storage recommended.
